category_title: Older preschool children expressing specific verbal and emotional concerns about transition
minimal_context: The student is standing outdoors near the fence observing moving trucks and estimating how many toys can fit inside.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Look at the moving trucks coming to move the building next door. How many toys do you think can fit on one truck?

STUDENT: 600! Or maybe 1000!

TUTOR: That is a lot of toys. Those trucks and movers will move out our things in the next few weeks.

STUDENT: Will we have the same teachers?

TUTOR: We will have to wait and see.

STUDENT: But will the new school have a backyard? What is going to happen to our playground after we move?

options.A: Reinforces that the staff and classmates will remain the same, explains that the new school will have a playground, and opens the floor for more questions.
options.B: Works on the recognition of body language and facial cues while, at the same time, labeling feelings about the moving trucks.
options.C: Closely monitors and addresses prevalent behavioral problems, including breaking rules and aggressive behavior, while standing outdoors near the fence.
options.D: Gives a warning at the time about the repetitive questions but does not discipline the student further during the outdoor observation.
